args.text: Particle that expresses a temporal relationship. This word has 5 forms, each of which corresponds to a tense; its exact meaning depends on the tense as well as the tense of the containing clause.

comment.text: All Elves are coal miners, whence this sense follows naturally. This is the Generic term for an Elf whose social status is unknown, as well as the term for Canaries.
Used solely in reference to The Chimney Man’s hibernation.
A time period equal in length to a full hibernation cycle of The Chimney Man, which is approximately one year.

comment.text: Used solely in reference to The Chimney Man’s hibernation.

Specifically, this word means ‘to deliver the result of some work (generally Coal) to one’s superiors’ and is used both by low-ranking Elves to describe the process of handing in the Coal one has mined at the end of every day, as well as by high-ranking Elves to report the amount of Coal mined by their subordinates to their superiors on a regular basis.
This specifically refers to a type of cloak worn only by Colliers and Master Carbonators.

group.text: This term refers to Elves of the class of Collier and above. Formally, only Elves who are themselves Colliers or higher are allowed to use
on its own to refer to a high-class Elf. See also

comment.text: Always capitalised, unless derogatory lowering is applied. This noun is never declined for number as there is, has been, and will only ever be one Chimney Man.
Lit. ‘in a certain place’; used as the subject of verbs that don’t have a semantic agent and can stand on its own with a (zero) copula to signify ‘there is’.

group.text: ‘in’ and a demonstrative pronoun, lit. ‘In this/that place’. The proximal pronoun is used to mean ‘here’, the medial pronoun to mean ‘there (medial)’, and the distal pronoun to mean ‘there (distal)’.

comment.text: The most important noun in Elvish, second only to The Chimney Man. This word and all words derived from it are always capitalised.
